lesson.menu_title: At the Doctor -- Saying How You Feel (should)
lesson.menu_desc: Say how you feel and give advice: I have a headache. You should rest. New words: doctor, headache, fever, sore throat, cough, tired, rest, medicine -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 18 -- At the Doctor: Saying How You Feel (should)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Érica Maria Machado Santarem | Aula 18 -- At the Doctor: Saying How You Feel

listenings.text: Hello. Let me tell you about my day. This morning I did not feel well. I had a headache and a sore throat. I was very tired. I think I have a small fever too. So I called the doctor. The doctor said: You should rest at home. You should drink a lot of water. You should not go to work today. You can take some medicine for the headache. I listened to the doctor. Now I am at home, in bed. I feel a little better. Tomorrow I hope my cough is gone.

Hello. This is the doctor. Let me give you some simple advice. When you have a cold, you should rest. You should drink warm water. You should not work too much. If you have a fever, you should stay at home. If you have a bad cough, you should see a doctor. Do not worry. With rest and a little medicine, you will feel better soon. Take care of yourself. Health comes first.

extra_audio.text: First, the doctor asks: How do you feel today? Second, I answer: I have a headache and a sore throat. Third, the doctor asks: Do you have a fever? Fourth, I say: Yes, a little. I am very tired. Fifth, the doctor says: You should rest and drink water.
